verdicts.title: 401 session-expiry re-persists the logged-out user's trips to localStorage (privacy wipe defeated by the same handler's emit)
verdicts.reasoning: Verified core.ts:82-157 clearAuthToken removeItem's the blob but never resets STATE.trips/expenses/budgets/tripDays/archivedTrips/categories, and the 401 branch (core.ts:269-284) calls it then emit(STATE_CHANGED) which fires the saveState subscriber (state.ts:438) → debounced _doSave JSON.stringify(STATE) (state.ts:363) re-writing A's trips back to the key just removed; downgraded from P1 because pullFromServer unconditionally reassigns STATE.trips (api.ts:183) so B never sees A's trips after their pull — the leak is a transient on-disk/first-paint window on a shared device, not persistent exposure or data loss.

verdicts.title: Trip-media hydration Maps (_mediaLoadedTrips/_mediaVersion/_pendingMedia) survive logout → shared-device media loss + hidden media on shared trips
verdicts.reasoning: grep confirms .clear() is NEVER called on the three module-level singletons (media.ts:27/47/80) and neither clearAuthToken (core.ts) nor profile.ts logout touches them, so on a shared trip after A→B on one device the stale _mediaLoadedTrips.has(T) makes pullFromServer skip fetchTripMedia (api.ts:317) leaving B on the []-placeholder (consequence a), and persistTripMedia's hydration gate (media.ts:274) is bypassed so a placeholder write with A's still-matching _mediaVersion clobbers the server media (consequence b) — genuine data-loss, though gated on the narrow shared-device + shared-trip + matching-version race.

verdicts.title: Warm-path 409 union-merge resurrects a peer device's deleted media item
verdicts.reasoning: _postTripMedia's 409 recovery (media.ts:128-159) re-merges via _mergeMediaField (media.ts:62-73), a pure union-by-key with no tombstone, and persistTripMedia/upsertTrip send the full current media snapshot on every edit (api.ts:499) so a local DELETE is just an absent key — when the write 409s the server's current copy (which still has the item) re-adds it, undoing the delete; real correctness bug on a hydrated trip under a concurrent-edit version conflict, bounded to resurrecting one media item.

verdicts.title: Per-trip POI preference keys (enabledPois / pillEpicenters / poiAnchoring) are never pruned when a trip is deleted
verdicts.reasoning: Live-trip permanent-delete (trip-controls.ts:174-207) prunes expenses/tripDays/settlements/budgets/fxOverridesByTrip[id] but not preferences.enabledPois/pillEpicenters/poiAnchoring, and the archived-trip permanent-delete (deleteArchivedTrip, handlers.ts:116-143 — the cited line 94 was actually the restore path, but the substance holds) prunes only archivedTrips; enabledPois IS written per-trip-id (HeroMap.tsx:564) and loadState (state.ts:189-207) only type-resets these maps, never GCs stale tripId keys — a real but cosmetic localStorage leak with no functional impact since ids are unique.

verdicts.title: cpiCache / rateCache are persisted but not validated or non-finite-healed on load (NaN can reach the inflation factor), unlike the sibling money maps
verdicts.reasoning: validateLoadedState (schemas.ts:198-225) shape-checks only the 8 arrays + activeTripId + user and loadState heals fxOverridesByTrip/manualRates for non-finite (state.ts:146-188) but never cpiCache/rateCache, which feed the identical calc via makeInflationFactor(cpiCache[cur]) (Insights.tsx:824/267); the asymmetry is real and makeInflationFactor only guards latestVal!==0 — an Infinity latestVal slips past to a NaN output factor (most interior NaNs are self-healed by `v || latestVal` / `base ?`), so a genuine-but-theoretical hardening gap given these caches come from server fetches, not user input.
